input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (which targets Android 15+ / API 35), edge-to-edge display is enforced by default, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach was deprecated in Android API 30 and removed in API 35.

The solution in .NET 10 is to use the new `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` or handle insets properly. 

For .NET 10 and Android 15, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach no longer works because edge-to-edge rendering is now mandatory. Instead, I should recommend using MAUI's built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` feature, which automatically adjusts the view when the keyboard appears, or implementing proper inset handling through AndroidX Core APIs to manage the keyboard's impact on the layout.
